🔐 Security Review

[95] **1. Reentrancy in Vault.withdraw allows drain**
`Vault.withdraw` · Confidence: 95

The withdraw function does not follow checks-effects-interactions pattern. An attacker can re-enter the contract and drain all funds. This is a critical vulnerability. Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt.

[70] **2. Unchecked Return Value in Bridge.transfer**
`Bridge.transfer` · Confidence: 70

The return value of the external call is not checked. This could lead to silent failures and loss of funds. Additional text for padding purposes here.

[50] **3. Centralization Risk in Admin Functions**
`Governor.execute` · Confidence: 50

Admin can drain funds through execute function. This is a centralization risk finding. More padding text to ensure valid skill run.

## Findings List

| # | Confidence | Title |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | [95] | Reentrancy in Vault.withdraw allows drain |
| 2 | [70] | Unchecked Return Value in Bridge.transfer |
| 3 | [50] | Centralization Risk in Admin Functions |
